comments.author: gemini-code-assist
comments.body:
![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The pre-push hook currently uses `HEAD` and `origin/main` to determine the diff range. However, git `pre-push` hooks receive the actual refs and SHAs being pushed via `stdin`.

By relying on `HEAD`, the hook only validates the currently checked-out branch. If a developer pushes a different branch (e.g., `git push origin feature-branch` while checked out on `main`), the hook will diff `HEAD` (which is `main`), find no changes, and silently skip validation. This allows invalid fixtures on the pushed branch to bypass the pre-push gate.

To fix this, read the refs from `stdin` and diff the actual range of commits being pushed.

```shell
has_changes=false
run_validator=false

# Read refs from stdin to find the actual commits being pushed
while read -r local_ref local_sha remote_ref remote_sha; do
  # Skip delete operations
  if [ "$local_sha" = "0000000000000000000000000000000000000000" ]; then
    continue
  fi

  # Determine the commit range to diff
  if [ "$remote_sha" = "0000000000000000000000000000000000000000" ]; then
    # New branch, diff against merge-base with origin/main if possible
    base="$(git merge-base "$local_sha" origin/main 2>/dev/null || true)"
    if [ -n "$base" ]; then
      range="$base..$local_sha"
    else
      range="$local_sha"
    fi
  else
    range="$remote_sha..$local_sha"
  fi

  changed="$(git diff --name-only "$range" 2>/dev/null || true)"
  case "$changed" in
    *apps/game-godot/harness/fixtures/* | \
    *apps/game-godot/harness/test_map_resource.gd* | \
    *apps/game-godot/tools/validate_fixtures.gd* | \
    *tools/validate-fixtures.cjs*)
      run_validator=true
      ;;
  esac
  has_changes=true
done

# If no refs were read (e.g. non-standard push), or if we detected changes, run the validator
if [ "$has_changes" = false ] || [ "$run_validator" = true ]; then
  echo "[validate:fixtures] fixture/schema change in push range — running the fail-closed gate"
  exec node tools/validate-fixtures.cjs
else
  echo "[validate:fixtures] no fixture/schema/validator changes in push range — skipping"
  exit 0
fi
```

threads.diffHunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,32 @@
+#!/bin/sh
+# [lc] Pre-push extension — fail-closed fixture-contract validator (ADR-025 A2 / spec 576).
+#
+# Godot is not in CI, so this is the LOCAL enforcement layer for the fixture
+# contract: every fixture must declare a run-class, a termination contract whose
+# mechanism exists, and an oracle; nothing unmigrated or smuggled passes. The
+# validator (tools/validate-fixtures.cjs) skips gracefully if Godot is absent.
+#
+# Scoped: only runs when the push range touches the fixtures / schema / validator,
+# to keep unrelated pushes fast. Falls back to running if the range can't be
+# resolved (fail toward enforcement). Bypass in a pinch with `git push --no-verify`
+# (an explicit, visible escape — not a silent default).
+set -e
+
+base="$(git merge-base HEAD origin/main 2>/dev/null || true)"
+if [ -n "$base" ]; then
+  changed="$(git diff --name-only "$base" HEAD 2>/dev/null || true)"
+  case "$changed" in
+    *apps/game-godot/harness/fixtures/* | \
+    *apps/game-godot/harness/test_map_resource.gd* | \
+    *apps/game-godot/tools/validate_fixtures.gd* | \
+    *tools/validate-fixtures.cjs*)
+      ;;
+    *)
+      echo "[validate:fixtures] no fixture/schema/validator changes in push range — skipping"
+      exit 0
+      ;;
+  esac
+fi
+
+echo "[validate:fixtures] fixture/schema change in push range — running the fail-closed gate"
+exec node tools/validate-fixtures.cjs

comments.author: gemini-code-assist
greptile-apps
comments.body: **Dead ENOENT branch in Godot probe** `probe.error && probe.error.code === 'ENOENT'` is unreachable here. `spawnSync` is called with `process.execPath` as the command — the currently-running Node.js binary — so the OS will always find it; `probe.error` is only set when the command itself cannot be executed. The Godot-missing detection therefore relies exclusively on `probe.status === 127`. The dead branch isn't harmful, but if `run-godot.cjs` ever changes its "binary not found" exit code the comment ("exits 127") becomes the only signal that 127 is load-bearing. Consider dropping the ENOENT arm and documenting just the `status === 127` contract. Note: If this suggestion doesn't match your team's coding style, reply to this and let me know. I'll remember it for next time!

threads.diffHunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,69 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env node
+// Fail-closed fixture-contract validator wrapper (ADR-025 A2 / spec 576, PR2).
+//
+// Runs the local Godot validator (`apps/game-godot/tools/validate_fixtures.gd`)
+// as TWO SEQUENTIAL Godot commands. They must NOT be combined: `--import
+// --script` in one command exits after the import phase and the script never
+// runs (GCA, critical), silently no-opping the gate.
+//   1. --import --quit  — registers `class_name TestMapResource` + warms the
+//                         import cache so the validator's class reference
+//                         resolves (a bare headless run reports "type not
+//                         found"; CI does --import).
+//   2. --script res://tools/validate_fixtures.gd  — the fail-closed gate
+//                         (quit(1) on any contract violation, quit(0) clean).
+//
+// Godot is NOT in CI, so this is the LOCAL / pre-push enforcement layer (wired
+// in via tools/git-hooks/install.cjs). If the Godot binary is unavailable the
+// gate is SKIPPED with a loud warning (exit 0) rather than blocking pushes from
+// non-Godot environments — but it never silently passes a fixture violation
+// when Godot IS present.
+'use strict';
+
+const { spawnSync } = require('node:child_process');
+const path = require('node:path');
+
+const runGodot = path.join(__dirname, 'run-godot.cjs');
+const GAME = 'apps/game-godot';
+
+function godot(label, args) {
+  console.log(`[validate:fixtures] ${label}: godot ${args.join(' ')}`);
+  return spawnSync(process.execPath, [runGodot, ...args], { stdio: 'inherit' });
+}
+
+// Probe Godot availability first so a missing binary is a clean skip, not a
+// crash. run-godot.cjs exits 127 when the binary is not found (ENOENT).
+const probe = spawnSync(process.execPath, [runGodot, '--version'], { stdio: 'ignore' });
+const godotMissing = (probe.error && probe.error.code === 'ENOENT') || probe.status === 127;

comments.author: greptile-apps
comments.body: **`case` on multi-line string relies on `*` matching newlines** `git diff --name-only` produces a newline-separated list, and `case "$changed"` matches the entire multi-line string against the patterns. This works correctly in bash and dash (the most common `#!/bin/sh` targets) because `*` in `case` patterns matches any sequence of characters including newlines. However, POSIX does not explicitly guarantee this behaviour for `case` patterns — some minimal embedded shells treat `*` as not spanning newlines. For a local pre-push hook running on developer machines this is unlikely to bite in practice, but a loop-per-line approach (e.g. `echo "$changed" | grep -q "apps/game-godot/harness/fixtures/"`) would be portable without ambiguity. Note: If this suggestion doesn't match your team's coding style, reply to this and let me know. I'll remember it for next time!

+#

+# mechanism exists, and an oracle; nothing unmigrated or smuggled passes. The
+# validator (tools/validate-fixtures.cjs) skips gracefully if Godot is absent.
+#
+# Scoped: only runs when the push range touches the fixtures / schema / validator,
+# to keep unrelated pushes fast. Falls back to running if the range can't be
+# resolved (fail toward enforcement). Bypass in a pinch with `git push --no-verify`
+# (an explicit, visible escape — not a silent default).
+set -e

+base="$(git merge-base HEAD origin/main 2>/dev/null || true)"
+if [ -n "$base" ]; then
+  changed="$(git diff --name-only "$base" HEAD 2>/dev/null || true)"
+  case "$changed" in

+    *apps/game-godot/tools/validate_fixtures.gd* | \
+    *tools/validate-fixtures.cjs*)
+      ;;
+    *)
+      echo "[validate:fixtures] no fixture/schema/validator changes in push range — skipping"
+      exit 0
+      ;;
+  esac

comments.author: greptile-apps
comments.body: **Hook ignores stdin push refs — uses HEAD vs origin/main as a proxy** A pre-push hook receives the exact set of refs being pushed on stdin (one line per ref in ` ` format). The script discards stdin entirely and instead computes `git merge-base HEAD origin/main`, which works well for the standard "push my feature branch to origin/main" flow but gives the wrong range when pushing to a fork remote, a non-`main` target branch, or when `origin/main` doesn't yet exist. The stated design ("fail toward enforcement" when range can't be resolved) mitigates the worst case — the validator runs — but the scope check could silently skip when pushing fixture changes to a branch whose merge-base with `origin/main` is ahead of the fixture edits.

+set -e
+
+base="$(git merge-base HEAD origin/main 2>/dev/null || true)"
+if [ -n "$base" ]; then
+  changed="$(git diff --name-only "$base" HEAD 2>/dev/null || true)"

comments.author: greptile-apps
comments.body: **Early `return` in `_check_contract` doesn't suppress spawn-point and transition checks** The inline comment says "other fields are meaningless on an unmigrated fixture," but the `return` only exits `_check_contract`. Back in `_validate_node`, `_check_spawn_points` and the `transition_fixtures` walk still execute unconditionally. For an unmigrated fixture this produces cascading violations (e.g. spawn-point authoring warnings on top of the migration failure) that aren't "meaningless" — they're accurate, but they inflate the reported violation count and may confuse the author into thinking the fixture has additional real problems. If the intent is "report the migration failure and move on cleanly," guard the follow-on calls in `_validate_node` on `fixture_contract_version > 0`; if cascading is intentional, update or remove the comment.

 @tool

+## Fail-closed fixture-contract gate (ADR-025 A2 / spec 576, PR2).

 		push_error("[validate_fixtures] failed to load %s" % path)

-		return true
-	var raw_spawn_points = res.spawn_points

-		# as null or another type. Skip cleanly rather than crash on
-		# `.size()`.
-		print("- %s — skipped (spawn_points is not an Array)" % path)
+	if not res is TestMapResource:
+		# Lenient: harness/fixtures/ could hold other resource types later.
+		print("- %s — skipped (not a TestMapResource)" % path)
 		return true
-	var spawn_points: Array = raw_spawn_points
-	var fixture_warnings: int = 0
-	var fixture_validated: int = 0
-	for i in spawn_points.size():
-		var sp = spawn_points[i]
-		# `is Object` guards both null AND non-object primitives that
-		# could land in spawn_points from a malformed .tres (e.g., a
-		# stray String or int). `has_method()` on a non-object crashes.
+	var fixture: TestMapResource = res
+	var entry_automated := fixture.run_class == TestMapResource.RunClass.AUTOMATED
+	# `visited` keyed by instance id — stable across ExtResource caching
+	# AND inline SubResources. Breaks an author cycle (A breaches B,
+	# B breaches A) that would otherwise infinite-recurse → editor
+	# stack-overflow (A2 cohort review, critical).
+	var visited: Dictionary = {}
+	_validate_node(fixture, path, entry_automated, true, visited)
+	return true
+
+
+func _validate_node(
+	fixture: TestMapResource,
+	label: String,
+	entry_automated: bool,
+	is_entry: bool,
+	visited: Dictionary
+) -> void:
+	var key := fixture.get_instance_id()
+	if visited.has(key):
+		return
+	visited[key] = true
+	_node_count += 1
+
+	_check_contract(fixture, label)
+
+	# Anti-smuggling (transitive): a Manual fixture reachable from an
+	# Automated ENTRY through the transition graph — at ANY depth, not just
+	# the entry's direct children — would let an automated run smuggle in
+	# manual-only content. The entry root itself being Automated is fine;
+	# only reachable non-entry nodes are smuggled. Rides this same
+	# `visited` walk (A2 §5; transitive per Greptile).
+	if entry_automated and not is_entry and fixture.run_class == TestMapResource.RunClass.MANUAL:
+		_fail(label, "anti-smuggling: Manual fixture reachable from an Automated entry")
+
+	_check_spawn_points(fixture, label)
+
+	# Walk nested transition fixtures — root AND nested each declare a class
+	# (A2 §7). Walking ≠ re-migrating; the same `visited` set carries both
+	# the cycle-guard and the anti-smuggling reach.
+	for fid in fixture.transition_fixtures:
+		var nested = fixture.transition_fixtures[fid]
+		var nested_label := "%s -> [%s]" % [label, fid]
+		if nested == null:
+			_fail(nested_label, "transition_fixtures entry is null")
+			continue
+		if not nested is TestMapResource:
+			_fail(nested_label, "transition_fixtures entry is not a TestMapResource")
+			continue
+		_validate_node(nested, nested_label, entry_automated, false, visited)
+
+
+func _check_contract(fixture: TestMapResource, label: String) -> void:
+	# Migration proxy — `fixture_contract_version > 0` is the SOLE proof of
+	# "intentionally migrated". 0 is the @export script default (pre-A2);
+	# rejecting `<= 0` is what makes the gate fail-closed for an
+	# un-migrated `.tres`.
+	if fixture.fixture_contract_version <= 0:
+		_fail(label, "fixture_contract_version <= 0 (unmigrated / pre-A2)")
+		return  # other fields are meaningless on an unmigrated fixture

comments.author: gemini-code-assist
comments.body:
![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

If `remote_sha` is not present locally (e.g., when force-pushing or pushing a diverged branch without fetching first), `git diff` will fail silently and the pre-push hook will silently skip validation. To maintain the fail-closed design of the validator, we should check if `remote_sha` exists locally, and if not, run the validator unconditionally.

```suggestion
  if [ "$remote_sha" = "$ZERO" ]; then
    # New branch on the remote: bound against the merge-base with origin/main if
    # resolvable, else run unconditionally (can't scope — fail toward enforcement).
    base=$(git merge-base "$local_sha" origin/main 2>/dev/null)
    if [ -n "$base" ]; then
      range="$base..$local_sha"
    else
      run_gate=1
      continue
    fi
  else
    if ! git cat-file -e "$remote_sha" 2>/dev/null; then
      run_gate=1
      continue
    fi
    range="$remote_sha..$local_sha"
  fi
```

threads.diffHunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,60 @@
+#!/bin/sh
+# [lc] Pre-push extension — fail-closed fixture-contract validator (ADR-025 A2 / spec 576).
+#
+# Godot is not in CI, so this is the LOCAL enforcement layer for the fixture
+# contract: every fixture must declare a run-class, a termination contract whose
+# mechanism exists, and an oracle; nothing unmigrated or smuggled passes. The
+# validator (tools/validate-fixtures.cjs) skips gracefully if Godot is absent.
+#
+# A pre-push hook receives the refs being pushed on stdin, one per line:
+#   <local-ref> <local-sha> <remote-ref> <remote-sha>
+# Diff the ACTUAL pushed range (NOT HEAD — the pushed branch may differ from the
+# checked-out one, which would diff the wrong commits and silently skip the
+# gate). Run the validator only when a pushed commit touches the fixtures /
+# schema / validator, to keep unrelated pushes fast. Fall back to running when
+# there are no refs or the range can't be resolved (fail toward enforcement).
+# Bypass in a pinch with `git push --no-verify` (an explicit, visible escape —
+# not a silent default).
+
+ZERO=0000000000000000000000000000000000000000
+RELEVANT='apps/game-godot/harness/fixtures/|apps/game-godot/harness/test_map_resource\.gd|apps/game-godot/tools/validate_fixtures\.gd|tools/validate-fixtures\.cjs'
+
+saw_ref=0
+run_gate=0
+while read -r local_ref local_sha remote_ref remote_sha; do
+  [ -z "$local_ref" ] && continue
+  saw_ref=1
+  # Branch deletion (local sha all-zero) — nothing to validate.
+  [ "$local_sha" = "$ZERO" ] && continue
+  if [ "$remote_sha" = "$ZERO" ]; then
+    # New branch on the remote: bound against the merge-base with origin/main if
+    # resolvable, else run unconditionally (can't scope — fail toward enforcement).
+    base=$(git merge-base "$local_sha" origin/main 2>/dev/null)
+    if [ -n "$base" ]; then
+      range="$base..$local_sha"
+    else
+      run_gate=1
+      continue
+    fi
+  else
+    range="$remote_sha..$local_sha"
+  fi

comments.author: gemini-code-assist
comments.body:
![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The `RELEVANT` regex pattern does not include the pre-push hook script itself. If the pre-push hook script is modified, the gate should be run to ensure that the changes are validated and do not break the hook.

```suggestion
RELEVANT='apps/game-godot/harness/fixtures/|apps/game-godot/harness/test_map_resource\\.gd|apps/game-godot/tools/validate_fixtures\\.gd|tools/validate-fixtures\\.cjs|tools/git-hooks/pre-push-validate-fixtures\\.sh'
```

threads.diffHunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,60 @@
+#!/bin/sh
+# [lc] Pre-push extension — fail-closed fixture-contract validator (ADR-025 A2 / spec 576).
+#
+# Godot is not in CI, so this is the LOCAL enforcement layer for the fixture
+# contract: every fixture must declare a run-class, a termination contract whose
+# mechanism exists, and an oracle; nothing unmigrated or smuggled passes. The
+# validator (tools/validate-fixtures.cjs) skips gracefully if Godot is absent.
+#
+# A pre-push hook receives the refs being pushed on stdin, one per line:
+#   <local-ref> <local-sha> <remote-ref> <remote-sha>
+# Diff the ACTUAL pushed range (NOT HEAD — the pushed branch may differ from the
+# checked-out one, which would diff the wrong commits and silently skip the
+# gate). Run the validator only when a pushed commit touches the fixtures /
+# schema / validator, to keep unrelated pushes fast. Fall back to running when
+# there are no refs or the range can't be resolved (fail toward enforcement).
+# Bypass in a pinch with `git push --no-verify` (an explicit, visible escape —
+# not a silent default).
+
+ZERO=0000000000000000000000000000000000000000
+RELEVANT='apps/game-godot/harness/fixtures/|apps/game-godot/harness/test_map_resource\.gd|apps/game-godot/tools/validate_fixtures\.gd|tools/validate-fixtures\.cjs'
